### claim (3)
- **Your editor and tool stack are not what make you fast.** — [ada-focus](https://example.com/ada/focus) `[CORE]`
- **The fastest people Ada knows use boring tools they have used for years.** — [ada-focus](https://example.com/ada/focus) `[STATED]`
- **Every new tool is a new thing to maintain and forget.** — [ada-focus](https://example.com/ada/focus) `[STATED]`

### concept (1)
- **Focus is a budget, not a feeling: you spend it on the work or on the meta-work.** — [ada-focus](https://example.com/ada/focus) `[CORE]`

### method (1)
- **Ada keeps one notebook, one editor, and one terminal, and almost never changes them.** — [ada-focus](https://example.com/ada/focus) `[CORE]`

### opinion (2)
- **The myth, which Ada rejects, that more tools mean more output.** _(quoted)_ — [ada-focus](https://example.com/ada/focus) `[STATED]`
- **The boring stack is the fast stack.** — [ada-focus](https://example.com/ada/focus) `[CORE]`

### warning (2)
- **Switching tools every month feels productive but is a tax you pay over and over.** — [ada-focus](https://example.com/ada/focus) `[STATED]`
- **Meta-work is sneaky because it looks like progress.** — [ada-focus](https://example.com/ada/focus) `[STATED]`
